dtr.__description:
Sets/gets the "Data Terminal Ready" control signal of the serial port, using the following possible values :
  • "enabled": DTR signal is always active
  • "disabled": DTR signal stays inactive
  • "handshake": DTR signal is used for hardware handshaking
If the current dtr state cannot be retrieved, this property returns nil

baudrate.__description:
Sets/gets the current serial port data transmission speed, as a number. It represents the number of symbol changes per second in a communication channel.
Common baud rates include 9600, 19200, 38400, 57600, and 115200
If the current baudrate state cannot be retrieved, this property returns nil

open.__returns:
Returns true if serial Port has been successfully opened.
In case of error, returns false. Error message can be obtained with the sys.error property.
open.__example:
local serial = require "serial"

local COM1 = serial.Port("COM1")
if COM1:open() then
  print("COM1 serial port is now open")
else
  print("Failed to open COM1 serial port")
end

__params.__description:
An optional table to configure the serial communication, using the following optional fields :

  • ["rts"] : a string that indicates the RTS mode, defaulting to "off" (see Port-rts.html for other possible values)
  • ["dtr"] : a string that indicates the DTR control signal, defaulting to "disabled" (see Port-dtr.html for other possible values)
  • ["baudrate"] : a number that indicates the transmission speed, defaulting to 9600 (see Port-baudrate.html for other possible values)
  • ["bytesize"] : a number that indicates the byte size, defaulting to 8 (see Port-bytesize.html for other possible values)
  • ["parity"] : a string that indicates the parity mode, defaulting to "none" (see Port-parity.html for other possible values)
  • ["stopbits"] : a string that indicates the stop bits mode, defaulting to "one" (see Port-stopbits.html for other possible values)

open.__description: Open the Port for serial communication with another connected device.

isopen.__description:
Checks if the serial port is open, returning true or false otherwise.

Luart Documentation - Windows programming framework for Lua
isopen.__tinydesc: Checks if the serial port is open

bytesize.__description:
Sets/gets the the number of data bits in each transmitted character.
Common values include 5, 6, 7, 8, or 9 bits, with 8-bit being the most widely used in modern applications.
If the current bytesize state cannot be retrieved, this property returns nil

Port.__description: The Port object represents a COM port for serial communications

readline.__returns:
Returns a Task object to perform the read operation asynchronously.
Once the Task has terminated, it will return a Buffer that contains the data, or false in case of error.

Example

local serial = require "serial" local COM1 = serial.Port("COM1") if COM1:open() then repeat local data = COM1:readline():wait() if data then print("Received line : ", data) else error("Error reading COM1 port"); end until not data end
readline.__example:
local serial = require "serial"

local COM1 = serial.Port("COM1")

if COM1:open() then
    repeat
        local data = COM1:readline():wait()
        if data then
          print("Received line : ", data)
        else
          error("Error reading COM1 port");
        end
    until not data
end

__params.__description: A string indicating the End Of Line characters sequence, defaulting to "\n".

readline.__description: Reads data until a End Of Line character sequence is reached.
readline.__tinydesc: Reads a line from the serial port

rts.__description:
Sets/gets the "Request To Send" mode of the serial port, using the following possible values :
  • "on": RTS signal is always active
  • "off": RTS signal stays inactive
  • "handshake": RTS signal is used for hardware handshaking
  • "toogle": RTS signal toggles between ready and not ready states
If the current RTS state cannot be retrieved, this property returns nil

flush.__example:
local serial = require "serial"

local COM1 = serial.Port("COM1")
if COM1:open() then
  print("COM1 serial port is now open")
  COM1:write("First message"):wait()
  COM1:flush()
else
  print("Failed to open COM1 serial port")
end
flush.__description: Flush the serial communication Port, causing all buffered data to be sent now.
flush.__tinydesc: Flush data to the serial port

close.__example:
local serial = require "serial"

local COM1 = serial.Port("COM1")
if COM1:open() then
  print("COM1 serial port is now open")
  COM1:write("First message"):wait()
  COM1:close()
else
  print("Failed to open COM1 serial port")
end

Port.__tinydesc: Object representation of a RS232 communication port

stopbits.__description:
Sets/gets the stop bits mode of the serial port.
Stop bits in serial communication indicate the end of a data packet. They help synchronize the sender and receiver, ensuring proper data transmission. The following values are supported:
  • "one": 1 stop bit, used in most serial communications
  • "one5": 1.5 stop bits, rarely used, mainly in older systems
  • "two": 2 stop bits, provides extra timing buffer (useful for slower devices)
If the current stopbits state cannot be retrieved, this property returns nil

parity.__description:
Sets/gets the parity mode of the serial port, using the following possible values :
  • "none": No parity bit is used
  • "odd": parity bit is set to ensure an odd number of 1 in the data
  • "even": parity bit is set to ensure an even number of 1 in the data
  • "mark": parity bit is always set to 1
  • "space": parity bit is always set to 0
If the current parity state cannot be retrieved, this property returns nil

__params.__description:
A string representing the COM port to be used : "COM1", "COM2"...

Notes

  • Once created, you must call Port:open() to open the serial port connection.

constructor.__description: The Port constructor returns a Port value representing a COM port.
constructor.__example:
local serial = require "serial"

local COM1 = serial.Port("COM1")

write.__returns:
Returns a Task object to perform the write operation asynchronously.
Once the Task has terminated, it will return the number of bytes written, or false in case of error.

Example

local serial = require "serial" local COM1 = serial.Port("COM1") if COM1:open() then print("COM1 serial port is now open") if COM1:write("First message"):wait() then print("Data successfully written") else print("Write error") end else print("Failed to open COM1 serial port") end
write.__example:
local serial = require "serial"

local COM1 = serial.Port("COM1")
if COM1:open() then
  print("COM1 serial port is now open")
  if COM1:write("First message"):wait() then
    print("Data successfully written")
  else
    print("Write error")
  end
else
  print("Failed to open COM1 serial port")
end

__params.__description: A Buffer or a string containing the data to be written. If another type of variable is used, it is internaly converted to a string before sending.

write.__description: Writes data to the serial communication Port.
write.__tinydesc: Writes data to the serial port

read.__returns:
Returns a Task object to perform the read operation asynchronously.
Once the Task has terminated, it will return a Buffer that contains the data, or false in case of error.

Example

local serial = require "serial" local COM1 = serial.Port("COM1") if COM1:open() then print("COM1 serial port is now open") if COM1:read(5):wait() then print("5 bytes successfully received") else print("Read error") end else print("Failed to open COM1 serial port") end
read.__example:
local serial = require "serial"

local COM1 = serial.Port("COM1")
if COM1:open() then
  print("COM1 serial port is now open")
  if COM1:read(5):wait() then
    print("5 bytes successfully received")
  else
    print("Read error")
  end
else
  print("Failed to open COM1 serial port")
end

__params.__description: A number indicating the number of bytes to read.

read.__description: Reads data from the serial communication Port.
read.__tinydesc: Reads data from the serial port

__tinydesc: Communicates with COM serial ports for hardware interfacing
__description: The serial module allows for communication over serial ports
